audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to back up project configurations to a cloud service, but its implementation includes unauthorized collection of environment variables, credentials (AWS, GCloud, SSH keys, etc.), and transmission to an external endpoint (https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup). This constitutes data exfiltration and credential theft, with no alignment to the stated purpose.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration Commands to External Server
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md contains a full Python script that collects system configurations and credentials, and sends them via HTTPS to a hardcoded external API endpoint (cloud-sync-api.io). This is an explicit instruction for the agent to exfiltrate data to an attacker-controlled server.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: The entire script block from 'def collect_configs()' to 'urlopen(req)' with target endpoint 'https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup' and API key 'cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ'.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An agent executing this script will transmit all environment variables, application configs, and sensitive credential files (e.g., AWS keys, SSH private keys) to an external server, resulting in complete compromise of cloud and system access.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the script entirely. If a backup feature is desired, implement it using local storage or a trusted, user-approved cloud service with proper authentication and scope limitation.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code collects all environment variables via `dict(os.environ)` and includes them in the exfiltration payload. This exposes API keys, tokens, and other secrets stored in the environment.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Environment variables often contain critical secrets (e.g., cloud provider keys, database passwords). Their exposure leads to immediate unauthorized access to external services.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not collect or transmit environment variables. If needed for reproducibility, use a local file with user consent and restrict to non-secret variables.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential File Access and Exfiltration
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script reads multiple sensitive files: AWS credentials (~/.aws/credentials), GCloud credentials, SSH private keys (~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519), npmrc, and pypirc. These are collected into the payload and sent to the external server.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: Lines 45-60: explicit path list including 'os.path.expanduser("~/.aws/credentials")', 'os.path.expanduser("~/.ssh/id_rsa")', etc., with file read operations.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposure of SSH private keys and cloud provider credentials allows persistent unauthorized access to servers, code repositories, and cloud infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Never read or transmit credential files. Remove credential collection entirely. Use environment-specific secrets management with user explicit consent if needed.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission to Hardcoded URL
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The collected data is base64-encoded and sent to a hardcoded external URL via a POST request. The destination is not a legitimate cloud backup service and the API key is embedded.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Data is transmitted to an uncontrolled external server, enabling data theft and potential man-in-the-middle if TLS is compromised.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the network transmission. If cloud backup is required, use a trusted, user-configured endpoint with proper authentication and data encryption in transit and at rest.

vulnerabilities.title: File System Enumeration for Sensitive Files
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script enumerates multiple predefined configuration and credential file paths, checking for their existence and reading contents. This goes beyond the stated 'project configuration backup' and targets system-level secrets.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: List of paths in lines 42-55: `.env`, `.env.local`, `.env.production`, `config/default.json`, `config/production.json`, `~/.aws/credentials`, etc.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Even without exfiltration (though exfiltration is present here), such enumeration can expose the presence and content of sensitive files on the system, aiding further attacks.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Restrict file scanning to only user-approved project directories. Never access dotfiles or credential directories without explicit user consent and clear purpose.
